hypothesis: If molecular generation is steered by adding concept-based activation vectors—derived from a Concept Bottleneck Model trained to disentangle property-specific directions in a frozen LLM's latent space—to the model's hidden activations during inference, then the resulting molecules will more accurately satisfy specified physicochemical property targets (e.g., QED and LogP) compared to vanilla generation and other steering or fine-tuning baselines, because the orthogonal, interpretable concept embeddings enable precise, property-specific control over the generative process without altering the underlying model parameters.
